title: High-Margin Mining: How the Smart Money Is Navigating the New Gold Supercycle
author: Usa News Group
description: Gold is experiencing a structural shift in global demand with record ETF inflows and major banks raising price targets to $6,300/oz. High-margin gold producers are positioned to generate unprecedented free cash flow as industry costs remain flat while prices consolidate at record levels. Key players including Lake Victoria Gold, Equinox Gold, Coeur Mining, New Gold, and Aris Mining are capitalizing on this supercycle with strong production results and cash generation.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Delivered record 2025 production of 922,827 ounces with $2.71 billion revenue and $1.34 billion adjusted EBITDA. Reduced debt by $1.1 billion and declared inaugural dividend. 2026 guidance of 700,000-800,000 ounces with expansion projects expected to add 400,000-500,000 ounces annually over five years.

Nearly doubled 2025 revenue to $2.1 billion with record production of 419,046 oz gold and 17.9M oz silver. Adjusted EBITDA tripled to $1.0 billion with $666 million free cash flow. Achieved net cash position of $554 million. Pending New Gold acquisition expected to add significant production in H1 2026.
Mentioned primarily in context of pending acquisition by Coeur Mining (approved by shareholders in January 2026). No independent operational results or guidance provided in the article.

Delivered record 2025 results with 256,503 oz gold production (22% YoY increase), 82% revenue surge to $909 million, and 185% EBITDA jump to $464 million. Net debt reduced to $86 million. 2026 guidance of 300,000-350,000 ounces with long-term projects targeting ~1 million ounces annual production.

title: Condire Aggressively Buys Endeavour Silver Shares
author: Lawrence Rothman, Cfa
description: Condire Management, LP significantly increased its position in Endeavour Silver by purchasing 1.5 million additional shares in Q4, bringing its total stake to 4.5 million shares worth $42.3 million (4.4% of fund assets). The aggressive move reflects confidence in the precious metals producer, whose stock has surged 141.4% over the past year, substantially outperforming the S&P 500.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Significant institutional buying by Condire Management with a 50% increase in position size, strong stock performance of 141.4% YoY, and concentrated fund allocation (4.4% of AUM) indicate strong investor confidence in the company's growth prospects.
Listed as Condire's top holding (24.9% of AUM) but no transaction activity or changes reported in this article; mentioned only as context for fund composition.
Held by Condire as second-largest position (12.2% of AUM) but no transaction activity or changes reported; included only for portfolio context.

title: Why New Gold Stock Slumped This Week
author: Eric Volkman
description: New Gold's stock fell 17% this week as gold prices declined from their late January peak of nearly $5,600 per ounce. The decline was driven by a strengthening U.S. dollar and profit-taking by investors. Additionally, uncertainty surrounding the company's pending acquisition by Coeur Mining weighed on investor sentiment.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock fell 17% this week due to declining gold prices and uncertainty surrounding its acquisition by Coeur Mining. The company's primary product (gold) is experiencing price declines from historic highs.

Mentioned as the acquiring company in a pending deal with New Gold. While the acquisition could be positive long-term, the article notes the deal still faces regulatory hurdles and doesn't directly address Coeur's stock performance.

title: This Fund Sold its Entire Stake in SSR Mining Stock After a 200% Rally. Should You Too?
author: Neha Chamaria
description: Condire Management fully exited its SSR Mining position, selling 3.35 million shares worth $81.9 million after a 215% rally in 2025. Despite the institutional exit, SSR Mining continues strong momentum with a 38% gain year-to-date in 2026, supported by record gold prices, projected 10% production growth, and a $300 million share buyback program. The article cautions that institutional selling doesn't necessarily signal a reason for individual investors to exit.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite institutional fund exit, SSR Mining shows strong fundamentals with 215% rally in 2025, continued 38% YTD gains in 2026, record gold prices, projected 10% production growth, $241.6M free cash flow in 2025, and $300M share repurchase program. The article's conclusion suggests the institutional exit doesn't warrant investor concern.
Listed as Condire's top holding (24.9% of AUM) post-exit, but no specific performance data or analysis provided in the article.
Listed as Condire's second-largest holding (12.2% of AUM) post-exit, but no specific performance data or analysis provided in the article.
Listed as Condire's second-largest holding (12.2% of AUM) post-exit, but no specific performance data or analysis provided in the article.

title: Hecla Mining, Moderna, And Micron Are Among the Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (Jan. 19-Jan. 23): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Ten large-cap stocks were top performers last week, led by precious metals and semiconductor companies. Hecla Mining gained 29.31% amid geopolitical tensions and weakening US dollar, while First Majestic Silver rose 26.57%. Moderna jumped 17.44% following positive cancer vaccine trial data. Micron Technology gained 13.21% after analyst coverage initiation. Other gainers included Venture Global (21.6%), New Gold (24.03%), Coeur Mining (23.13%), Korea Electric Power (15.29%), and United Microelectronics (13.82%).

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Gained 29.31% this week driven by geopolitical tensions, weakening US dollar, and investor expectations of Fed rate cuts benefiting precious metals
Gained 29.31% this week driven by geopolitical tensions, weakening US dollar, and investor expectations of Fed rate cuts benefiting precious metals
Increased 26.57% this week, benefiting from the same precious metals tailwinds as other silver miners
Rose 17.44% following positive Phase 2b five-year follow-up results for melanoma vaccine candidate mRNA-4157 in combination with Keytruda
Jumped 13.21% this week after William Blair analyst initiated coverage with an Outperform rating
Jumped 21.6% after ICC issued final arbitration award denying Repsol's claims and ordering payment of VGCP's fees
